author: w3Lb
content: Found by the #99 comparative benchmarks: on a 64 MiB single-file replace, ripsed takes ~6.3s vs sed's ~1.0s. Dominant cost: the undo log stores a JSON-escaped copy of the ENTIRE original text per modified file — for a 64 MiB file that's a second 64+ MiB serialize+write on every edit (with the 0600 chmod), plus the engine's per-line String materialization. PROPOSAL: (a) add undo.max_file_bytes config (default e.g. 4 MiB): files larger than the cap get no undo entry, with a one-line stderr note ('undo skipped for big.txt: exceeds undo.max_file_bytes') and --undo-list showing the gap; (b) add --no-undo flag for bulk runs; (c) consider storing undo entries as separate files instead of JSONL-escaped strings (avoids escape blowup, enables streaming write). Re-run cargo xtask bench-compare big-file after to quantify. Secondary (separate concern): engine line-Vec materialization for huge files — measure before optimizing.
